title: LLM-Based Examination of Eligibility Criteria from Securities Prospectuses at the German Central Bank
abstract: arXiv:2606.27316v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Verifying the eligibility of securities as collateral is a key responsibility of the German Central Bank. However, manually verifying these assets against legal and financial criteria within lengthy, semi-structured, and often bilingual prospectuses is a resource-intensive task. While previous efforts utilized traditional Named Entity Recognition (NER) for information extraction, these methods can struggle with OCR noise, linguistic variance, and rigid span-based constraints, and the need for manually annotated training data for each relevant annotation type. In this paper, we present the first case study applying Large Language Models (LLMs) to the eligibility examination process, shifting the paradigm toward a generative Information Extraction pipeline. Our approach decomposes the task into extraction, normalization, and interpretation, allowing for greater flexibility in handling noisy text and interleaved German-English content. We f

translated_title: 基于大语言模型的德国央行证券说明书资格标准审查
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.27316v1 公告类型：新 摘要：验证证券作为抵押品的资格是德国央行的一项关键职责。然而，在冗长、半结构化且通常为双语的说明书中，手动对照法律和财务标准核查这些资产是一项资源密集型任务。虽然以往的工作利用传统的命名实体识别（NER）进行信息提取，但这些方法难以应对OCR噪声、语言变异和严格的基于跨度的约束，并且需要为每种相关标注类型手动标注训练数据。在本文中，我们首次展示了将大语言模型（LLMs）应用于资格审查流程的案例研究，将范式转向生成式信息提取流水线。我们的方法将任务分解为提取、标准化和解释三个步骤，从而在处理噪声文本和交错出现的德英内容时具有更大的灵活性。
tldr: 本文首次将大语言模型应用于德国央行证券抵押品资格审查，通过生成式信息提取流水线分解任务，解决了传统方法在噪声文本和多语言处理上的局限性。
en_tdlr: This paper presents the first case study of applying LLMs to securities eligibility examination at the German Central Bank, using a generative information extraction pipeline to overcome limitations of traditional NER in handling noisy text and multilingual content.
